overview: Dimir Terror is a Pauper tempo deck that uses low-cost creatures, cheap disruption, and card selection to delay opponents and maintain board control. It relies on a graveyard-heavy cantrip package to efficiently cast powerful threats like Tolarian Terror and Gurmag Angler. The deck adapts its posture between aggressive and reactive lines depending on the matchup, aiming to outmaneuver opponents with mana-efficient interaction and protected win conditions.

keep_criteria: Hand contains a decent number of cantrips
Has at least one interaction spell or threat
Keep any hand with 2-3 lands (including duals/shocks) and at least one turn-1 or turn-2 play.
Prioritize hands with *Counterspell* or *Snuff Out* paired with a land.
Keep hands with *Lorien Revealed* only if you have a land and a way to protect it or a threat to support it.
mulligan_criteria: Hand is mismatched to known opponent (e.g., full of Snuff Out vs Burn)
Lacks lands or cantrips to find answers
Relies exclusively on cantrips to find initial interaction without Preordain
Mulligan pure land hands, hands with only high-cost spells, or hands with no interaction against aggro/combo.

examples.reason: Contains multiple cantrips, two lands, and interaction/threats, meeting the criteria for a safe keep against an unknown opponent.

early_game: Play lands and cast cheap cantrips like Brainstorm and Consider to filter the deck
Use low-cost disruption like Spell Pierce and Snuff Out to delay opponent's plans
Avoid over-committing to cantrips if it delays finding necessary interaction against aggressive decks
If you have *Counterspell* and can afford to hold priority, cast it
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast *Snuff Out* if an early threat appears, or play a land.
Sequence lands to ensure blue and black mana availability for *Snuff Out* (UB) and *Counterspell* (UU).
mid_game: Cast Tolarian Terror or Gurmag Angler once the graveyard is sufficiently populated
Protect threats with counterspells and removal to maintain board control
Adopt a reactive posture against explosive aggro to stabilize, or act first against value midranges
Preserve mana for *Counterspell* until the opponent reveals their spell.
Use *Snuff Out* on high-impact threats to maintain card parity.
Avoid overcommitting to *Lorien Revealed* without protection
Manage your graveyard by avoiding unnecessary exile effects unless facing graveyard-dependent decks.
Hold back on committing multiple threats until you have established a *Counterspell* or *Snuff Out* in hand.
late_game: Leverage 2-for-1 engines like Deep Analysis and Chainer's Edict for card advantage
Use sideboarded sweepers and targeted removal to handle resilient threats
Maintain pressure or control depending on matchup, ensuring opponent cannot outvalue the deck
*Lorien Revealed* combined with a board of creatures, or a well-timed *Snuff Out* on a game-ending threat.
If combo is present, *Counterspell* on the win condition.
Late-game card advantage from *Snuff Out* and *Lorien Revealed* usually closes out slow decks.
Go all-in when you have established card advantage, the opponent has no interaction, or you're facing a slow deck that can't recover from board loss.
Commit when you can protect your threats with *Counterspell* or *Snuff Out*, or when you're ahead in life and can trade efficiently.

key_cards.name: Tolarian Terror
key_cards.notes: Discounted by instants and sorceries in graveyard, protects itself well, and provides a four-turn clock.

key_cards.name: Gurmag Angler
key_cards.notes: Discounted by graveyard size, offers a 5/5 body for low mana, but requires careful graveyard management to avoid counterintuitive interactions with Terror.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.notes: Infinitely useful for discarding useless cards and setting up combos with Thought Scour or Deep Analysis.

key_cards.name: Thought Scour
key_cards.notes: Adds three cards to graveyard to speed up Terror or Angler, but risks milling threats.

key_cards.name: Consider
key_cards.notes: Preferred over Mental Note for safety; removes a useless card from the top while interacting with other cantrips.

key_cards.name: Spell Pierce
key_cards.notes: Solid answer in a mana-intensive format; protects Terror by making removal cost four additional mana.

key_cards.name: Snuff Out
key_cards.notes: Can be cast for free, allowing proactive board control or threat protection.

key_cards.name: Cast Down
key_cards.notes: Best two-mana black removal in Pauper, handles almost anything without protection or hexproof.

sequencing_tips: Measure graveyard size carefully before casting Gurmag Angler to balance discount with Tolarian Terror's needs.
Use Brainstorm to discard dead cards and set up Thought Scour or Deep Analysis for maximum efficiency.
Avoid relying solely on cantrips early game to find interaction, as it costs mana and delays development.
Pair removal on Tolarian Terror with Spell Pierce to force opponents to pay four extra mana to answer it.
Adopt an aggressive posture against midranges to prevent them from growing, but slow down against explosive aggro to stabilize.
*Lorien Revealed* is both a threat and a card draw engine
don't treat it as a static. *Snuff Out* is your primary removal and card advantage tool
use it wisely. *Counterspell* is your most important card
never cast it unless you're sure it will hit or you have a backup plan.
Watch for graveyard hate, as it can neutralize your value engines.
Common mistake: overcommitting to *Lorien Revealed* without protection, leading to a dead hand.

matchups.advice: Play slightly faster to avoid Armadillo Cloak turning the game around; sideboard extra Chainer's Edict.
Slow their clock initially to stabilize; be cautious with Deep Analysis due to life loss; sideboard Hydroblast and Unexpected Fangs.
Act first and respond later; protect threats early to prevent their card advantage engines from hitting the board.
Sideboard extra Dispel for Game 2; expect longer games where late-game safety valves matter.
Use Duress to disrupt early-game plans and check for Gurmag Angler timing; sideboard Hydroblast.
Sideboard Suffocating Fumes and Arms of Hadar to clear boards and extend sweeper reach.
Sideboard Nihil Spellbomb to punish graveyard reliance and cycling strategies.

Use *Snuff Out* on their counterspell if it's a creature, or play around it by casting threats at end step. Use *Lorien Revealed* to apply pressure that forces them to use multiple counters. Consider *Thoughtseize* to remove key counters before they can be cast.

Prioritize *Snuff Out* on mana dorks or acceleration spells. Use *Counterspell* on their big finishers. *Lorien Revealed* can stabilize against their early threats. Don't let them establish a mana advantage; trade efficiently and punish overextensions.

Keep *Counterspell* for their best early threats. Use *Snuff Out* on any creature that reaches 2+ power. *Lorien Revealed* is key for stabilization; sacrifice it for draw if needed. Don't overextend; focus on survival and out-grinding them in the late game.

Trade resources efficiently. Use *Snuff Out* on their removal. Apply pressure with *Lorien Revealed* to force mistakes. Use *Counterspell* to protect your key spells. Out-value them in the late game by leveraging *Snuff Out*'s card advantage engine.

Win the resource war. Use *Snuff Out* on their *Lorien Revealed* or key threats. *Counterspell* is king; hold it for their win conditions. *Thoughtseize* to disrupt their hand. Prioritize graveyard hate if they rely on it. Play conservatively and punish overextensions.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, discard spells, and removal.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: this is a Tempo deck similar to the classic Dimir Delver — low-cost creatures with a fast clock, cheap or free disruption, card selection and counterspells with a small number of lands — and your objective is to delay the opponent's plans and keep the board clean for enough turns to win the game.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: These are your threats, with only four copies of each... Tolarian Terror wants a graveyard full of Instants and Sorceries... Gurmag Angler, on the other hand, wants your graveyard full as soon as possible to feed on it directly

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: it is necessary to measure how much you need that certain number of Instants and Sorceries in your graveyard before casting it for the lowest cost benefit
Brainstorm becomes infinitely more useful than Preordain or Ponder due to its interaction with other spells, allowing for a setup where you can get rid of useless cards from your hand.
relying exclusively on them to find the initial interaction tends to be a bad idea, especially without Preordain.
along with Tolarian Terror, any removal on that creature alongside Spell Pierce now costs 4 more mana
you don't want to give the Midranges too much time to grow... while also not rushing too fast against very explosive Aggros

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: such as against Bogles, Boros Synthesizer, Affinity, Burn, Mono Red Blitz, etc.

against Caw-Gate, or other Basilisk Gate decks... against Stompy, Moggwarts, and White Weenie... against Izzet Faeries, Affinity or Jund Cascade... punishing strategies like Boros Bully, Familiars, and Cycling Storm.
